mnemonics: SARI-MA: Seasonal ARIMA Model. Remember the order: Seasonal (P,D,Q) and Non-Seasonal (p,d,q) for a complete model specification.
keyPoints: Parsimonious models use a few parameters to capture complex seasonal patterns, often through seasonal differencing (D) and seasonal AR (P) or MA (Q) terms.
The Multiplicative Seasonal ARIMA model is a cornerstone of parsimonious seasonal modeling, combining non-seasonal and seasonal components efficiently.
A key principle is to first remove the seasonal component through differencing (e.g., at lag 12 for monthly data) to achieve stationarity before modeling the remaining structure.
commonMistakes: Overfitting the model by using high-order seasonal AR or MA terms instead of a simpler, more parsimonious structure.
Forgetting to check for and account for multiple seasonal cycles (e.g., both weekly and yearly patterns) in the data.
Applying seasonal differencing when it is not necessary, which can artificially create complex patterns and lead to model misspecification.
